moods.label: Bright
moods.description: Open, clear harmony with lifted scale color.

moods.label: Dark
moods.description: Minor gravity, close tension, and shadowed color.

moods.label: Jazzy
moods.description: Extended harmony, dominant motion, and modal color.

moods.label: Bluesy
moods.description: Blue-note friction with strong dominant character.

moods.label: Latin
moods.description: Rhythmic minor color with firm dominant pull.

moods.label: Film Noir
moods.description: Ambiguous minor harmony with chromatic suspense.

moods.label: Ethereal
moods.description: Suspended, spacious harmony with a weightless upper edge.

moods.label: Happy
moods.description: Direct major warmth with familiar consonant movement.

moods.label: Melancholy
moods.description: Reflective minor color without maximum darkness.

moods.label: Heroic
moods.description: Broad major harmony with decisive dominant motion.

moods.label: Ancient
moods.description: Modal, open-ended harmony with an old-world contour.

moods.label: Lively
moods.description: Forward motion with bright dominant and modal energy.

scaleLearning.label: Major (Ionian)
scaleLearning.useItFor: clear tonal melodies · familiar cadences · bright resolution
scaleLearning.useItOver: major triads, major-seventh chords, and diatonic progressions

scaleLearning.label: Dorian
scaleLearning.useItOver: minor-seventh chords when the natural sixth is available

scaleLearning.label: Phrygian
scaleLearning.useItFor: dark riffs · flamenco color · flat-second tension
scaleLearning.useItOver: minor chords and suspended riffs that emphasize the flat second

scaleLearning.label: Lydian
scaleLearning.useItFor: bright cinematic lift · floating major harmony
scaleLearning.useItOver: major-seventh sharp-eleven chords and tonic major drones
scaleLearning.hearItIn: Flying in a Blue Dream — Joe Satriani

scaleLearning.label: Mixolydian
scaleLearning.useItOver: dominant-seventh chords and major progressions with a flat seventh

scaleLearning.label: Natural Minor (Aeolian)
scaleLearning.useItOver: minor triads, minor-seventh chords, and natural-minor progressions

scaleLearning.label: Locrian
scaleLearning.useItOver: half-diminished chords and unresolved diminished-tonic textures

scaleLearning.label: Harmonic Minor
scaleLearning.useItFor: neoclassical metal · flamenco · dramatic minor-key tension
scaleLearning.useItOver: minor tonic chords and dominant-to-minor resolutions

scaleLearning.label: Locrian natural 6
scaleLearning.useItOver: half-diminished chords when a natural sixth supports the line

scaleLearning.label: Ionian sharp 5
scaleLearning.useItOver: augmented-major chords and major tonal centers with a sharp fifth

scaleLearning.label: Dorian sharp 4
scaleLearning.useItOver: minor-sixth chords and minor vamps with a raised fourth

scaleLearning.label: Phrygian Dominant
scaleLearning.useItFor: flamenco · surf rock · dominant flat-nine color
scaleLearning.useItOver: dominant flat-nine chords, flamenco dominants, and V chords in minor

scaleLearning.label: Lydian sharp 2
scaleLearning.useItFor: bright altered harmony · exotic major color
scaleLearning.useItOver: major-seventh sharp-eleven chords with an augmented second

scaleLearning.label: Altered Diminished
scaleLearning.useItFor: diminished tension · altered resolution · modern jazz
scaleLearning.useItOver: diminished-seventh chords and altered leading-tone resolutions

scaleLearning.label: Melodic Minor
scaleLearning.useItFor: jazz minor harmony · ascending minor melodies
scaleLearning.useItOver: minor-major-seventh and minor-sixth tonic chords

scaleLearning.label: Dorian flat 2
scaleLearning.useItOver: minor-seventh chords with a flat ninth and natural sixth

scaleLearning.label: Lydian Augmented
scaleLearning.useItOver: major-seventh sharp-five chords and augmented tonic colors

scaleLearning.label: Lydian Dominant
scaleLearning.useItOver: dominant-seventh sharp-eleven chords and tritone substitutions

scaleLearning.label: Mixolydian flat 6
scaleLearning.useItFor: dominant chords with minor-sixth color · film noir
scaleLearning.useItOver: dominant-seventh chords that resolve into minor harmony

scaleLearning.label: Locrian natural 2
scaleLearning.useItOver: half-diminished chords in minor ii–V progressions

scaleLearning.label: Altered Scale
scaleLearning.useItFor: altered dominants · maximum tension before resolution
scaleLearning.useItOver: altered dominant chords carrying flat and sharp fifth or ninth tensions

scaleLearning.label: Major Pentatonic
scaleLearning.useItOver: major and dominant chords when a consonant five-note palette is wanted

scaleLearning.label: Minor Pentatonic
scaleLearning.useItFor: rock riffs · blues solos · minor vamps
scaleLearning.useItOver: minor chords, power-chord riffs, and dominant blues harmony

scaleLearning.label: Major Blues
scaleLearning.useItOver: major-key blues and dominant chords with passing blue-note color

scaleLearning.label: Minor Blues
scaleLearning.useItFor: electric blues · rock leads · blue-note tension
scaleLearning.useItOver: minor blues, dominant blues, and rock progressions
scaleLearning.hearItIn: The Thrill Is Gone — B.B. King

scaleLearning.label: Hungarian Minor
scaleLearning.useItFor: dramatic folk color · metal · two augmented seconds
scaleLearning.useItOver: minor chords and pedal tones that support raised-fourth and leading-tone tension

scaleLearning.label: Whole Tone
scaleLearning.useItOver: augmented triads and dominant chords suspended from tonal resolution

scaleLearning.label: Diminished (whole–half)
scaleLearning.useItOver: diminished-seventh chords and symmetrical passing movement
